posts._note: Hook: This book is for people who want to become X. Stop the scroll. Claim the reader's identity.
posts.linkedin:
This book is for the couple who still loves each other.

And is quietly terrified that love might not be enough.

You are not in a bad relationship.
You are in an unmaintained one.

And there is a difference.

A bad relationship needs to end.
An unmaintained one needs a system.

Relationship Maintenance gives you that system.

Not a list of tips.
Not another conversation about communication styles.
Not a retreat or a therapist's waitlist.

A 45-minute weekly protocol that keeps two people genuinely known to each other — through careers, children, financial pressure, grief, and the relentless ordinary weight of a shared life.

Built around the W.E.E.K. Framework.
Backed by four decades of relationship science.
Written for couples who are serious about what they are building.

If you have ever looked across the table at the person you chose and felt the distance without being able to name it —

This book was written for that moment.

Get it now at publixion.com

#RelationshipMaintenance #MarriageAdvice #CouplesGoals #Relationships #Publixion

posts._note: Why Publixion built this. The origin story. What the system solves. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
We built this book because the relationship self-help genre has a problem.

It is enormous, well-intentioned, and almost entirely built for couples who are already in crisis.

Walk into any bookshelf and you will find titles about surviving affairs, recovering from betrayal, rebuilding after deception.

These books are necessary. For the couples who need them, they are lifelines.

But they are emergency rooms.

Not gyms.

They treat the wound after it has opened rather than building the strength that might have prevented it.

We wanted to build the gym.

A system — not a sentiment.
A protocol — not a principle.
Something with the same reliable logic as a workout program, where a couple can look at week three and know exactly what to do, why it matters, and what measurable change to expect.

So we spent months researching four decades of relationship science — Gottman, Johnson, Fisher, Perel, Aron — and built the W.E.E.K. Framework around what the data actually shows keeps couples strong.

Not passion. Not compatibility. Not luck.

Intentionality made structural.

That is Relationship Maintenance.
And it is available now at publixion.com

#RelationshipMaintenance #Publixion #CouplesSystem #MarriageSystem #RelationshipScience
posts.facebook:

Not therapy. Not a retreat. A system.

posts._note: Bold contrarian statement from the book. Designed to spike engagement and challenge assumptions.
posts.linkedin:
Hot take: Date nights do not save marriages.

I know. It is the advice everyone gives.

Struggling with your partner? Book a babysitter. Go to dinner. Reconnect.

And yes — dinner is lovely. But dinner once a month is a gesture, not a system. It addresses the symptom without touching the structure.

Here is what the research actually shows:

Couples who schedule regular, structured check-in conversations — not dates, not activities, not passive time together — report relationship satisfaction scores 3.5 times higher than couples who rely on spontaneous connection.

3.5 times.

Not because the conversation is magic. Because it is consistent. Because it makes both people's emotional reality visible to each other week by week, before the invisible distance becomes the kind of distance that requires a crisis to name.

Date nights are a deposit.
Weekly check-ins are the account.

You need both. But one of them is keeping the relationship alive. And it is not the one with the reservation.

Relationship Maintenance is built around the practice that actually works. Get it at publixion.com

#RelationshipMaintenance #MarriageTruths #CouplesAdvice #RelationshipScience #Publixion

posts._note: A powerful passage or story from the book. Give free value. Build reciprocity.
posts.linkedin:
This is a real passage from Chapter 1 of Relationship Maintenance.

Read it slowly.

---

Drift is not the loud enemy of love. It is the quiet one. It does not arrive with warning signs or ultimatums. It arrives dressed as routine, as busyness, as 'we'll make time for that later.' It arrives looking exactly like a normal life. And by the time most couples recognize it, they are already standing in a house that feels like it belongs to two polite strangers who used to know each other very well.

Most couples do not fail from explosive crises. They fail from slow drift. And the difference between the couples who reverse it and those who do not is not passion, compatibility, or the absence of hard circumstances. It is intentionality made structural.

---

If that sentence landed somewhere real for you, the book is at publixion.com

The Drift Diagnostic in Chapter 1 takes ten minutes and will tell you exactly where you are standing right now.

#RelationshipMaintenance #DriftEffect #LongTermRelationship #MarriageAdvice #Publixion

posts._note: Why this is different from everything else in the category. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Here is every relationship book on the market right now:

Category 1: Crisis rescue books.
For couples surviving betrayal, addiction, or serious rupture. Necessary and important. Not what most couples need.

Category 2: Theory-heavy academic translations.
Rigorously researched. Written like a graduate textbook. Puts down the principles and leaves the reader to figure out application.

Category 3: The date-night list.
Gentle, optimistic, forgettable by Tuesday. Long on suggestion, short on structure.

None of these categories gives a couple what they actually need: a system.

Not inspiration. Not information. A repeatable, structured, weekly operating protocol that works in ordinary life — under work pressure, with small children, in the weeks where love feels like a logistical partnership more than a chosen intimacy.

Relationship Maintenance is that system.

It is built around the W.E.E.K. Framework — a four-component 45-minute weekly protocol grounded in four decades of relationship science. It includes communication scripts, conflict resolution templates, an intimacy planning framework, an emotional debt audit, a repair ritual design guide, and a 30-day implementation plan.

Not principles. Protocols.
Not insight. Tools.
Not a better version of what already exists. Something the category has been missing.

Get it at publixion.com

#RelationshipMaintenance #MarriageSystem #CouplesTools #Publixion #RelationshipBooks

posts._note: Answer 5 buying hesitations directly and honestly. Q&A format.
posts.linkedin:
Five questions we get about Relationship Maintenance. Answered honestly.

Q: Our relationship is not in crisis. Is this book for us?
A: It was written specifically for you. The majority of readers describe their relationship as good or fine — and privately feel that fine is not what they signed up for. This is a maintenance system, not a rescue kit. It is most powerful before the damage is visible.

Q: My partner is resistant to relationship books. What do I do?
A: Start with Chapter 2. Ask your partner to agree to 45 minutes of structured conversation — not a book club, a conversation. Most resistant partners are engaged participants by week three, when the results speak for themselves.

Q: How much time does this actually require each week?
A: 45 minutes for the weekly check-in. 5 to 8 minutes of daily micro-practices. Under two hours total across a full week. Most couples spend more time than that on a single episode of television they watch together.

Q: We already argue a lot. Will this make it worse?
A: The first four weeks can surface tensions that have been building without a container. This is not the system making things worse — it is the system giving existing issues somewhere to go. Chapter 4's C.A.L.M. framework is built specifically for this transition period.

Q: How long before we see a real difference?
A: Most couples report a measurable shift in emotional connection within three to four weeks of consistent practice. The Drift Diagnostic, retaken at Day 30, typically shows a 15 to 30 point improvement.

Get the book at publixion.com

#RelationshipMaintenance #CouplesAdvice #MarriageSystem #Publixion
posts.facebook:
The five questions we hear most about Relationship Maintenance:

Q: Our relationship is fine. Is this for us?
A: Fine is exactly who this is for. Maintenance works best before the crisis.

Q: My partner will not read relationship books.
A: Do not frame it as a book. Frame it as a 45-minute weekly conversation you want to try for a month.

Q: How much time does it take?
A: 45 minutes per week for the core practice. Less time than one episode of most shows you are currently watching.

Q: Will it make our arguments worse at first?
A: It may surface things that have been sitting unaddressed. The book includes a specific protocol for exactly this.

Q: How fast will we see results?
A: Most couples report feeling genuinely closer within three to four weeks of consistent practice.

Get it at publixion.com
